_comment: SHAPE sweep on the game's default map. Question is not 'how big does it get' but 'how does the rule-set SPEND the map': how many cities, how much land worked, and how much of that land no city can reach at any price (the city-less carpet). Axes are the levers from the marginal-cap + dessert rework: kappa sets the viability cliff at C=kappa*c (EXTENT), desserts/premium/displace set market reach and how much grain a luxury burns, newCoreMinFarmers gates town ignition. dessertX fixed at 3 so premium*X (the frontier multiplier) stays in the sane 1.5-2.4 band. urbanize:true is REQUIRED — with it off no city ever ignites and every shape metric is meaningless. One neutral 'probe' strategy so each axis reads as a clean parameter. Run: node harness.js sweep_planet_shape.json
